text.title: Generative UI Design Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the generative-ui-design methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Generated UI MUST be labelled ideation-only; consumers MUST NOT use it as a production handoff.
At least 5 variants MUST be generated per brief.
Brand tokens + must-not-have list MUST be injected in the prompt, not added post-hoc.
Variant selection MUST be human, with a recorded rationale.
When the workflow is agent-driven (no human in the keyboard), Claude Artifacts MUST be used.
rule.rationale: Other generators require a human at the keyboard.

rule.statement: When preconditions hold, run the full procedure end-to-end and emit the output artefact.
rule.rationale: Routes positive decision-tree leaves to the procedure.

rule.statement: When preconditions fail or skip-conditions hold, exit without producing the artefact and document why.
rule.rationale: Routes negative decision-tree leaves out without polluting the output channel.
